sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jack, a 30-year-old male, presents with a complaint of neck pain that began approximately a week ago following a car accident.
He reports that his neck went forward and his head went forward quickly, resulting in pain.
The pain is not constant but worsens with movement of the neck.
He rates the pain as a 7 out of 10.
He has tried ibuprofen, which provided some relief but not significant improvement.
He has also experienced headaches, which have been present lately.
The pain is localized to the back of the neck and does not radiate to the upper back or shoulders.
Past medical history: The patient has no mention of past medical conditions, treatments, or encounters.
Review of systems: The patient denies any hearing problems or visual disturbances.
He has a history of playing football years ago but has not experienced any issues with his neck.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]

Physical exam: The patient has positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, positive pain with flexion, severe pain with extension, positive pain with rotation, and positive pain with lateral bending.
Test Results: The results of the x-ray show no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has neck strain.
Problem list: Neck strain; Active
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for inflammation, and Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I advised the patient to rest and avoid straining his neck.
I ordered an MRI to rule out any nerve impingement.
I referred the patient to either physical rehab or a chiropractor to further manage his condition.
Follow-up: I will review the results of the MRI and follow up with the patient accordingly.
